name: West Coast Wilderness Trail
description: A relatively accessible trail ride through West Coast scenery.
difficultyExplanation: It follows a purpose-built trail corridor with clear navigation and regular accommodation nearby. Flat to rolling terrain and frequent service access make it approachable.
beAwareOf: Weather changes, wind exposure, and changing trail conditions can all materially affect daily progress.
tips: Start early, carry a weather margin, and keep backup navigation plus an extra food and water buffer.
trickiestSection: The Hokitika Gorge swing bridge section is the most technically committing - narrow, exposed, and impassable in flood.
routeSummary: West Coast Wilderness Trail is a easy bikepacking route covering about 129 km, commonly ridden over 2 days. A relatively accessible trail ride through West Coast scenery. Riders can expect 60% Gravel / 35% Road / 5% Track as the route unfolds.

Good planning makes this route far more enjoyable. The best riding window is generally Year-round, with resupply described as Carry enough food and water for longer gaps between settlements; verify opening hours before committing to remote sections.. Overnight options are typically A mix of camping, huts, and local stays depending on your pace and daily target distance..

The key section to manage is: The Hokitika Gorge swing bridge section is the most technically committing - narrow, exposed, and impassable in flood. Keep a close eye on Weather changes, wind exposure, and changing trail conditions can all materially affect daily progress., especially if conditions change during the day.
recommendedSetupText: For this 2-day route, we recommend a Gravel Bike to balance comfort and control across mixed terrain. This setup keeps key gear stable and easy to access over long days.
